docs.title: Euro-Office Documentation
docs.text:
Your sovereign office

Euro-Office is a self-hostable document server — documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and PDFs — designed to integrate with a platform such as Nextcloud.

It is not a standalone application; it requires a compatible storage backend to provide the user interface and file storage.

  • Quickstart

    Get Euro-Office running and connected to your platform in minutes.

    Install with Docker

  • Install

    Docker, Ubuntu (deb), and Fedora (rpm) installation guides.

    Installation guide

  • Develop

    Build Euro-Office from source, run the dev environment, contribute.

    Developer setup

  • Build

    Build the Docker image and distribution packages from source.

    Building from source

docs.title: What's inside
docs.text:
• Introduction — what Euro-Office is and how its pieces fit together.
  • Installation — production deployment via Docker, Ubuntu (deb), and Fedora (rpm).
  • Integration — connecting Euro-Office to Nextcloud and other DMS platforms.
  • Development — building Euro-Office from source.

docs.title: Building from source
docs.text: Euro-Office builds entirely inside Docker via docker buildx bake. The only host requirements are Docker with the buildx container driver, and Git with SSH access to the repositories.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before building for the first time, complete the one-time setup described in build/BUILD_REQUISITES.md:

  • Docker CE installed (not podman — RHEL-based systems default to podman)
  • Your build user added to the docker group and re-logged in

  • A buildx container driver created and bootstrapped:

    docker buildx create \
  --name container-builder \
  --driver docker-container \
  --use

docker buildx inspect --bootstrap

  • SSH key added to your GitHub profile (for submodule access)

docs.title: Clone the repository
docs.text:
git clone --recurse-submodules git@github.com:Euro-Office/DocumentServer.git
cd DocumentServer

If you already cloned without --recurse-submodules:

git submodule update --init --recursive

docs.title: Build the Docker image
docs.text:
All build commands run from the build/ subdirectory:

cd DocumentServer/build
BUILDX_BAKE_ENTITLEMENTS_FS=0 docker buildx bake --allow=fs.read=.. --load

This builds the standalone target (the default group) and loads the resulting image into your local Docker daemon as ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest.

Why these flags:

Flag Reason BUILDX_BAKE_ENTITLEMENTS_FS=0 Newer BuildKit versions gate filesystem access behind entitlement checks. This disables the check since the access is explicitly granted below. --allow=fs.read=.. The build reads source files from the parent directory (context = ".." in docker-bake.hcl). BuildKit requires explicit permission for this. --load Imports the finished image into the local Docker daemon. Without this flag the image is built but not accessible via docker run.

Verify the image was created:

docker images ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver

docs.title: Run and verify the image
docs.text:
Start the locally built image:

docker run -d \
  --name euro-office-dev \
  --restart=unless-stopped \
  -p 8080:80 \
  -e JWT_ENABLED=true \
  -e JWT_SECRET=dev-secret \
  ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest

Wait for the container to finish initialising, then check the health endpoint:

curl http://localhost:8080/healthcheck

Expected output: true

To open the built-in example app in a browser:

docker run -d \
  --name euro-office-dev \
  --restart=unless-stopped \
  -p 8080:80 \
  -e JWT_ENABLED=true \
  -e JWT_SECRET=dev-secret \
  -e EXAMPLE_ENABLED=true \
  ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest

Then open http://localhost:8080/example/ in your browser.

Stop and remove the container when done:

docker stop euro-office-dev && docker rm euro-office-dev

docs.title: Build a branded image
docs.text:
The default build produces a Euro-Office image. To build for a different brand (e.g. Nextcloud Office), include the brand-specific HCL file alongside the main bake file:

cd DocumentServer/build
BUILDX_BAKE_ENTITLEMENTS_FS=0 docker buildx bake \
  -f docker-bake.hcl \
  -f brands/nextcloud-office-brand/brand-server.hcl \
  --allow=fs.read=.. \
  --allow=fs=./brands/nextcloud-office-brand/generated \
  --load

The additional --allow=fs=./brands/nextcloud-office-brand/generated flag grants BuildKit write access to the directory where the brand asset generation step exports its output.

What changes in a branded build:

Default (Euro-Office) Branded (Nextcloud Office) Image tag ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest ghcr.io/nextcloud-office/documentserver:latest Install path inside image /var/www/euro-office/ /var/www/nextcloud-office/ COMPANY_NAME env var Euro-Office Nextcloud Office Visual assets Built-in defaults Generated from brands/nextcloud-office-brand/svg/

How the brand asset pipeline works:

Including a brand HCL overrides the dummy brand-icons target in docker-bake.hcl with a real build step. That step runs build-icons.bake.Dockerfile — an Alpine container with rsvg-convert — which renders the five source SVGs into PNGs, ICOs, and correctly named SVG copies for each component (server admin panel, welcome page, example app). The generated files are written to brands/nextcloud-office-brand/generated/ and then injected into the server, example, and packages build stages via COPY --from=brand-icons.

Customising the visual identity:

The visual output depends entirely on the five source SVGs in brands/nextcloud-office-brand/svg/:

svg/
  logo-dark.svg        # square app icon, dark theme
  logo-light.svg       # square app icon, light theme
  logo-large-dark.svg  # wide logo, dark theme
  logo-large-light.svg # wide logo, light theme
  splash.svg           # splash screen

Replace these files with your own artwork and rebuild. Everything in generated/ is derived from them — do not edit the generated files directly.

Note

The nextcloud-office-brand source SVGs currently contain the same artwork as the Euro-Office defaults. A branded build will compile and run correctly, but will look identical until different SVG files are placed in svg/.

docs.title: Build targets
docs.text: Target / group Command Output standalone (default) docker buildx bake ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest develop docker buildx bake develop ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest-dev — includes build tools packages docker buildx bake packages .deb and .rpm files in build/deploy/packages/ cluster docker buildx bake cluster Orchestrated cluster images (cluster-docs, cluster-example, cluster-utils)

docs.title: Override the version
docs.text:
The version is read from the VERSION file at the repository root. Override it at build time via environment variables:

PRODUCT_VERSION=9.3.1 BUILD_NUMBER=42 \
  BUILDX_BAKE_ENTITLEMENTS_FS=0 \
  docker buildx bake --allow=fs.read=.. --load

BUILD_NUMBER is appended to the version string (e.g. 9.3.1-42). It defaults to dev.1 when not set.

docs.title: Build distribution packages
docs.text:
cd DocumentServer/build
BUILDX_BAKE_ENTITLEMENTS_FS=0 docker buildx bake packages \
  --allow=fs.read=.. \
  --allow=fs=./deploy/packages

Packages are written to build/deploy/packages/. Both .deb and .rpm are produced for each architecture in a single run:

deploy/packages/
  euro-office-documentserver_9.3.1-dev.1_arm64.deb
  euro-office-documentserver-9.3.1-dev.1.aarch64.rpm

The --allow=fs=./deploy/packages flag grants BuildKit write access to that output directory.

Output directory is not cleared between runs

The deploy/packages/ directory accumulates packages across builds. Remove old packages before building if you want only the current run's output:

rm -rf deploy/packages && mkdir -p deploy/packages

Note

The packages target builds the full source first (same pipeline as standalone) before producing the packages. Expect the same build time as a full image build on a cold cache.

docs.title: Override version and build number
docs.text:
PRODUCT_VERSION=9.3.1 BUILD_NUMBER=42 \
  BUILDX_BAKE_ENTITLEMENTS_FS=0 \
  docker buildx bake packages \
  --allow=fs.read=.. \
  --allow=fs=./deploy/packages

This produces euro-office-documentserver_9.3.1-42_<arch>.deb and the equivalent .rpm.

docs.title: Cache layer lock errors
docs.text:
When building with a warm cache, multiple targets may attempt to write the same cache layer in parallel, producing errors like:

ERROR: (*service).Write failed: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = ref layer-sha256:... locked

These are transient and BuildKit retries them automatically — the build completes successfully. If the errors cause an actual build failure, disable cache writes for that run:

BUILDX_BAKE_ENTITLEMENTS_FS=0 docker buildx bake \
  --allow=fs.read=.. \
  --load \
  --set "*.cache-to="

docs.title: Broken build cache
docs.text:
If a build stalls or fails with errors about missing paths inside the build cache (e.g. cannot change to '/build-cache1/third_party/workdir/icu/icu'), prune the BuildKit cache and retry:

docker buildx prune -a

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Euro-Office accepts contributions through pull requests against the relevant component repository. The canonical guide currently lives in DocumentServer/CONTRIBUTING.md; the points below are a summary.

docs.title: Before you start
docs.text:
• For non-trivial changes, open an issue first to align on approach.
  • Read AGENTS.md if you are using AI-assisted tools — there are specific commit-trailer requirements.

docs.title: Code style
docs.text:
• 120-character line width.
  • 4-space indentation.
  • Commits are signed (DCO) and follow Conventional Commits v1.0.0.

docs.title: Pull request requirements
docs.text:
• Tests for new behavior (Jest, Mocha, or Chai depending on the subpackage).
  • Documentation updates for user-visible changes.
  • Two approvals before merge.

Coming soon

The full content of CONTRIBUTING.md will be migrated into this page, plus contributor onboarding for first-time contributors.

docs.title: Developer setup
docs.text: The full Euro-Office stack runs locally via the docker-compose-based environment in DocumentServer/develop/. It builds each component from source, exposes hot-reload paths for the editor UI, and includes a paired Nextcloud instance for end-to-end testing.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker 24+ with buildx.
  • 16 GB RAM minimum (the first build is heavy).
  • Git with SSH access to https://github.com/Euro-Office.
  • A Linux x86_64 or arm64 host. macOS works for everything except native-core C++ debugging.

docs.title: Clone and bootstrap
docs.text:
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer.git
cd DocumentServer/develop
./scripts/up.sh        # placeholder — exact entrypoint TBD

docs.title: Layout of the dev environment
docs.text:
The compose file spins up:

  • Euro-Office document server (built from local source).
  • Nextcloud (with the Euro-Office integration app pre-installed).
  • PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ.
  • An nginx front-end with self-signed TLS.

docs.title: Mobile / non-localhost testing
docs.text:
When testing from a phone or a separate machine, override the public URL via the docker-compose .env so the editor connects through your LAN IP.

Coming soon

The full reference for the develop environment, migrated from DocumentServer/develop/README.md. That document is currently the source of truth.

docs.title: Installation Guide
docs.text: Euro-Office Document Server can be installed in several ways depending on your environment and requirements.

docs.title: Choose your installation method
docs.text:
• Ubuntu (deb)

    Install from a .deb package on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS. Suitable for bare-metal and VMs.

    Ubuntu installation

  • Docker

    Run the official container image. Quickest way to get started.

    Docker installation

  • Debian (deb)

    Install from a .deb package on Debian 12 (Bookworm).

    Debian installation

  • Fedora (rpm)

    Install from an .rpm package on Fedora 41+. Tested on Fedora 44.

    Fedora installation

docs.title: Verify your installation
docs.text:
Once installed, use the built-in example app to confirm the editor works end-to-end in a browser.

Testing with the example app

docs.title: Which method should I use?
docs.text: Docker Ubuntu (deb) Debian (deb) Fedora (rpm) Recommended for production Yes Yes Yes Easiest to update Yes Full OS control Yes Yes Yes Nextcloud integration Yes Yes Yes Yes

docs.title: Installing Euro-Office on Debian
docs.text: This guide covers installing Euro-Office Document Server on Debian 12 (Bookworm) from a GitHub release package.

docs.title: System requirements
docs.text:
• Debian 12 (Bookworm) — arm64 or amd64
  • 10 GB disk space minimum
  • 4 GB RAM minimum

docs.title: Step 1 — Enable the contrib component
docs.text:
The ttf-mscorefonts-installer package required by Euro-Office lives in Debian's contrib component, which is not enabled by default. Edit /etc/apt/sources.list and add contrib to each line:

deb http://deb.debian.org/debian bookworm main contrib
deb http://deb.debian.org/debian bookworm-updates main contrib
deb http://deb.debian.org/debian-security/ bookworm-security main contrib

Then update the package index:

sudo apt-get update

docs.title: Step 2 — Install prerequisites
docs.text:
All prerequisites are available in the default Debian 12 repositories — no extra package sources required:

sudo apt-get install -y postgresql redis-server rabbitmq-server nginx supervisor

docs.title: Step 3 — Create the database
docs.text:
The post-install script connects to PostgreSQL during installation. Create the user and database first:

sudo -u postgres psql -c "CREATE USER ds WITH PASSWORD 'ds';"
sudo -u postgres psql -c "CREATE DATABASE ds OWNER ds;"

docs.title: Step 4 — Pre-seed the installer answers
docs.text:
echo "ds ds/db-type select postgres
ds ds/db-host string localhost
ds ds/db-port string 5432
ds ds/db-user string ds
ds ds/db-pwd password ds
ds ds/db-name string ds" | sudo debconf-set-selections

docs.title: Step 5 — Download the package
docs.text:
Download the latest release from GitHub Releases:

# Replace <version> and <arch> with your values, e.g. 9.3.1-rc.1 and amd64 or arm64
wget "https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer/releases/download/v<version>/euro-office-documentserver_<version>_<arch>.deb" \
  -O /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb

Available architectures: amd64, arm64

docs.title: Step 6 — Install the package
docs.text:
sudo apt-get install -y /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb

The installer generates fonts, WOPI keys, and JS caches. This takes a minute. A successful install ends with:

Congratulations, the Euro-Office DocumentServer has been installed successfully!

docs.title: Step 7 — Verify
docs.text:
Check that all services are running:

systemctl is-active ds-docservice ds-converter ds-metrics nginx

Expected output:

active
active
active
active

Run the health check:

curl http://localhost/healthcheck

Expected output: true

docs.title: Optional: Test with the built-in example app
docs.text: To verify the editor works end-to-end in a browser, follow the Example App guide.

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
Download the new release package and reinstall:

wget "https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer/releases/download/v<new-version>/euro-office-documentserver_<new-version>_<arch>.deb" \
  -O /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb
sudo apt-get install -y /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
sudo apt-get remove --purge euro-office-documentserver
sudo -u postgres psql -c "DROP DATABASE ds;"
sudo -u postgres psql -c "DROP USER ds;"

docs.title: Installing Euro-Office via Docker
docs.text: The quickest way to run Euro-Office Document Server is via the official Docker image.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker Engine 20.10 or later
  • 5 GB disk space for the image
  • 4 GB RAM minimum

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
docker run -d \
  --name euro-office \
  --restart=unless-stopped \
  -p 80:80 \
  -e JWT_ENABLED=true \
  -e JWT_SECRET=your-secret \
  ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest

The server is ready when the health check returns true:

curl http://localhost/healthcheck

docs.title: Image tags
docs.text:
Tag Use latest Most recent release — use in production nightly Nightly builds from main — not for production latest-dev Development image with build tools included

Pin to a specific version in production:

ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:9.3.1

docs.title: Verify with the example app
docs.text:
To test the editor in a browser, enable the built-in example app:

docker run -d \
  --name euro-office \
  --restart=unless-stopped \
  -p 8080:80 \
  -e JWT_ENABLED=true \
  -e JWT_SECRET=your-secret \
  -e EXAMPLE_ENABLED=true \
  ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest

Then open http://localhost:8080/example/ in your browser.

Warning

Disable EXAMPLE_ENABLED in production. The example app has no access control.

docs.title: Persistent data
docs.text:
By default, documents and configuration are lost when the container is removed. Mount volumes to persist them:

docker run -d \
  --name euro-office \
  --restart=unless-stopped \
  -p 80:80 \
  -e JWT_ENABLED=true \
  -e JWT_SECRET=your-secret \
  -v /path/to/data:/var/lib/euro-office/documentserver \
  -v /path/to/logs:/var/log/euro-office/documentserver \
  -v /path/to/config:/etc/euro-office/documentserver \
  ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text: Variable Default Description JWT_ENABLED true Enable JWT authentication JWT_SECRET — Shared secret — set this in production JWT_HEADER Authorization HTTP header carrying the JWT EXAMPLE_ENABLED false Enable the built-in example app WOPI_ENABLED false Enable WOPI protocol support ALLOW_PRIVATE_IP_ADDRESS false Allow document server to fetch from private IPs NGINX_WORKER_PROCESSES 1 Number of nginx worker processes GENERATE_FONTS true Regenerate font cache on startup DB_HOST localhost PostgreSQL host (for external DB) DB_NAME eurooffice PostgreSQL database name DB_USER eurooffice PostgreSQL user REDIS_SERVER_HOST localhost Redis host (for external Redis) AMQP_HOST localhost RabbitMQ host (for external RabbitMQ)

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
docker pull ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest
docker stop euro-office && docker rm euro-office
# re-run with the same docker run command

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
docker stop euro-office
docker rm euro-office
docker rmi ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver:latest

docs.title: Testing with the built-in example app
docs.text: Euro-Office ships a Node.js example application that lets you open and edit documents in the browser. It is intended for testing and verification only — do not expose it on a public server.

docs.title: Step 1 — Start the example service
docs.text:
sudo systemctl start ds-example
systemctl is-active ds-example

Expected output: active

docs.title: Step 2 — Configure the example app
docs.text:
The example app needs to know its own URL so the document server can fetch and save files correctly. Edit /etc/euro-office/documentserver-example/local.json:

sudo tee /etc/euro-office/documentserver-example/local.json > /dev/null << 'EOF'
{
  "server": {
    "siteUrl": "/",
    "exampleUrl": "http://YOUR_SERVER_IP/example",
    "token": {
      "enable": true,
      "secret": "REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_JWT_SECRET",
      "authorizationHeader": "Authorization"
    }
  }
}
EOF

Replace YOUR_SERVER_IP with the server's IP address or hostname. Do not add a trailing slash.

localhost vs server IP

If you are running the server directly on your local machine (not in a container or VM), you can use http://localhost/example as the exampleUrl. In all other cases — including LXD/LXC containers, VMs, and remote servers — use the actual IP address. Using localhost in those environments causes the document server to generate malformed callback URLs.

Get the JWT secret:

deb install (Ubuntu)rpm install (Fedora)

The installer generates a JWT secret automatically. Look it up with: 

sudo grep -A1 '"browser"' /etc/euro-office/documentserver/local.json | grep '"string"'

Use the secret you set manually in Step 9 of the Fedora installation guide.

Then restart the example service:

sudo systemctl restart ds-example

docs.title: Step 3 — Open the example in your browser
docs.text:
http://<your-server-ip>/example/

You should see a file list. Click Create to open a blank document in the editor.

docs.title: Running on a non-standard port
docs.text:
If nginx is configured to listen on a port other than 80, update two settings:

1. nginx listen port — edit /etc/euro-office/documentserver/nginx/ds.conf:

listen 0.0.0.0:<port>;
listen [::]:<port> default_server;

2. exampleUrl — update /etc/euro-office/documentserver-example/local.json:

"exampleUrl": "http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:<port>/example"

Then reload nginx and restart the example:

sudo systemctl reload nginx
sudo systemctl restart ds-example

docs.title: Disabling the example app
docs.text:
sudo systemctl stop ds-example
sudo systemctl disable ds-example

docs.title: Installing Euro-Office on Fedora
docs.text:
This guide covers installing Euro-Office Document Server on Fedora 41 or later from a GitHub release RPM package.

Tested on Fedora 44

These steps have been verified end-to-end on Fedora 44. Rocky Linux 9 is not supported due to a glibc version incompatibility — see Known issues.

docs.title: System requirements
docs.text:
• Fedora 41 or later (x86_64 or aarch64)
  • 10 GB disk space minimum
  • 4 GB RAM minimum

docs.title: Step 1 — Install prerequisites
docs.text:
Enable the RabbitMQ packagecloud repository:

curl -1sLf 'https://packagecloud.io/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-server/script.rpm.sh' | sudo bash

Install all prerequisites:

sudo dnf install -y \
  postgresql-server postgresql postgresql-contrib \
  valkey \
  rabbitmq-server \
  nginx \
  supervisor

Initialize PostgreSQL, then start and enable all services:

sudo postgresql-setup --initdb
sudo systemctl enable --now postgresql valkey rabbitmq-server nginx supervisor

docs.title: Step 2 — Configure PostgreSQL authentication
docs.text:
Fedora's PostgreSQL uses ident authentication by default, which blocks password-based logins. Edit /var/lib/pgsql/data/pg_hba.conf and change ident to md5 on the two 127.0.0.1 and ::1 lines:

# before
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            ident
host    all             all             ::1/128                 ident

# after
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            md5
host    all             all             ::1/128                 md5

Reload PostgreSQL:

sudo systemctl reload postgresql

docs.title: Step 3 — Create the database
docs.text:
sudo -u postgres psql -c "CREATE USER ds WITH PASSWORD 'ds';"
sudo -u postgres psql -c "CREATE DATABASE ds OWNER ds;"

docs.title: Step 4 — Download the package
docs.text:
Download the latest RPM from GitHub Releases:

# Replace <version> with your value, e.g. 9.3.1-rc.1
wget "https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer/releases/download/v<version>/euro-office-documentserver-<version>.x86_64.rpm" \
  -O /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.rpm

Available architectures: x86_64, aarch64

docs.title: Step 5 — Install the package
docs.text:
The msttcore-fonts package is not available in Fedora's repositories. Install with --nodeps to skip that dependency:

sudo rpm -ivh --nodeps /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.rpm

docs.title: Step 6 — Initialize the database schema
docs.text:
The RPM installer does not run the database schema automatically. Apply it manually:

sudo -u postgres psql -d ds \
  -f /var/www/onlyoffice/documentserver/server/schema/postgresql/createdb.sql
sudo -u postgres psql -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public TO ds;"
sudo -u postgres psql -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA public TO ds;"

docs.title: Step 7 — Fix the nginx configuration
docs.text:
Fedora's /etc/nginx/nginx.conf requires two changes before the document server can serve requests.

1. The conf.d directory is not included. Find this line in the http {} block:

# include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf;

Uncomment it:

include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf;

2. A default server block intercepts port 80. Comment out the entire server {} block in the same file that listens on port 80 (it starts with listen 80; and typically spans 15–20 lines):

# server {
#     listen       80;
#     listen       [::]:80;
#     server_name  _;
#     root         /usr/share/nginx/html;
#     ...
# }

Test and reload:

sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

docs.title: Step 8 — Install OpenSSL and generate JS caches
docs.text:
The cache-generation script requires openssl, which is not installed on Fedora by default:

sudo dnf install -y openssl
sudo /usr/bin/documentserver-flush-cache.sh

Without this step the editor will fail to load with a path error like Cannot GET /9.3.1-/web-apps/….

docs.title: Step 9 — Configure JWT authentication
docs.text:
The RPM installer does not generate a JWT configuration. Create /etc/euro-office/documentserver/local.json with a secret of your choice — all three entries must use the same value:

sudo tee /etc/euro-office/documentserver/local.json > /dev/null << 'EOF'
{
  "services": {
    "CoAuthoring": {
      "token": {
        "enable": {
          "request": { "inbox": true, "outbox": true },
          "browser": true
        },
        "secret": {
          "inbox": { "string": "REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_SECRET" },
          "outbox": { "string": "REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_SECRET" },
          "browser": { "string": "REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_SECRET" }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
EOF

Note the secret — you will need it when configuring the example app.

docs.title: Step 10 — Start the document server services
docs.text: sudo systemctl enable --now ds-docservice ds-converter ds-metrics

docs.title: Step 11 — Verify
docs.text:
Check that all services are running:

systemctl is-active ds-docservice ds-converter ds-metrics nginx

Expected output:

active
active
active
active

Run the health check:

curl http://localhost/healthcheck

Expected output: true

docs.title: Optional: Test with the built-in example app
docs.text:
To verify the editor works end-to-end in a browser, follow the Example App guide.

Use the server IP, not localhost

When configuring the example app on Fedora, set exampleUrl to the server's actual IP address rather than localhost (e.g. http://192.168.1.10/example). Using localhost causes malformed callback URLs.

Since you set the JWT secret manually in Step 9, skip the grep command and enter that same secret directly.

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
Download the new release RPM and upgrade in place:

wget "https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer/releases/download/v<new-version>/euro-office-documentserver-<new-version>.x86_64.rpm" \
  -O /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.rpm
sudo rpm -Uvh --nodeps /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.rpm
sudo /usr/bin/documentserver-flush-cache.sh

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
sudo rpm -e euro-office-documentserver
sudo -u postgres psql -c "DROP DATABASE ds;"
sudo -u postgres psql -c "DROP USER ds;"

docs.title: Rocky Linux 9 not supported
docs.text:
The RPM package is built on Ubuntu with glibc 2.35. Rocky Linux 9 ships glibc 2.34 — one minor version behind. This causes the font and JS generation step (AllFonts.js) to fail silently, leaving the editor broken with no obvious error.

Rocky Linux 10 (which ships with a newer glibc) may work once it is available. Until then, use Fedora 41+ or the Docker installation instead.

docs.title: Installing Euro-Office on Ubuntu
docs.text: This guide covers installing Euro-Office Document Server on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (Noble) from a GitHub release package.

docs.title: System requirements
docs.text:
• Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (arm64 or amd64)
  • 10 GB disk space minimum
  • 4 GB RAM minimum

docs.title: Step 1 — Install prerequisites
docs.text:
Euro-Office requires PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Nginx, and Supervisor. Install them before the package:

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y postgresql redis-server rabbitmq-server nginx supervisor

docs.title: Step 2 — Create the database
docs.text:
The post-install script connects to PostgreSQL during installation. Create the user and database first:

sudo -u postgres psql -c "CREATE USER ds WITH PASSWORD 'ds';"
sudo -u postgres psql -c "CREATE DATABASE ds OWNER ds;"

docs.title: Step 3 — Pre-seed the installer answers
docs.text:
The package installer asks for database connection details non-interactively. Pre-seed the answers so the post-install script can configure itself without a prompt:

echo "ds ds/db-type select postgres
ds ds/db-host string localhost
ds ds/db-port string 5432
ds ds/db-user string ds
ds ds/db-pwd password ds
ds ds/db-name string ds" | sudo debconf-set-selections

docs.title: Step 4 — Download the package
docs.text:
Download the latest release from GitHub Releases:

# Replace <version> and <arch> with your values, e.g. 9.3.1-rc.1 and amd64 or arm64
wget "https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer/releases/download/v<version>/euro-office-documentserver_<version>_<arch>.deb" \
  -O /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb

Available architectures: amd64, arm64

docs.title: Step 5 — Install the package
docs.text:
sudo apt-get install -y /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb

The installer will generate fonts, WOPI keys, and JS caches. This takes a minute. A successful install ends with:

Congratulations, the Euro-Office DocumentServer has been installed successfully!

docs.title: Step 6 — Verify
docs.text:
Check that all services are running:

systemctl is-active ds-docservice ds-converter ds-metrics nginx

Expected output: 

active
active
active
active

Run the health check:

curl http://localhost/healthcheck

Expected output: true

docs.title: Optional: Test with the built-in example app
docs.text: To verify the editor works end-to-end in a browser, follow the Example App guide.

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
Download the new release package and reinstall:

wget "https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer/releases/download/v<new-version>/euro-office-documentserver_<new-version>_<arch>.deb" \
  -O /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb
sudo apt-get install -y /tmp/euro-office-documentserver.deb

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
sudo apt-get remove --purge euro-office-documentserver
sudo -u postgres psql -c "DROP DATABASE ds;"
sudo -u postgres psql -c "DROP USER ds;"

docs.title: Nextcloud
docs.text: Euro-Office has first-class Nextcloud integration via a dedicated Nextcloud app that connects to a running Euro-Office document server.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A running Euro-Office document server reachable from your Nextcloud instance — see installation.
  • The JWT secret you configured in your document server, which you'll need to enter in the Nextcloud app settings.
  • Nextcloud 34 or later

docs.title: Install the Nextcloud app
docs.text:
1. In Nextcloud, go to Apps → Office & text → Euro-Office integration.
  2. Click Download and enable.

docs.title: Configure the app
docs.text:
1. In Nextcloud, go to Settings → Administration → Euro-Office.
  2. Enter the URL of your Euro-Office document server, including the protocol and port (e.g. https://eurooffice.example.com).
  3. Enter the JWT secret you configured in your document server.
  4. Click Save.

docs.title: Source
docs.text: The Nextcloud integration app lives at https://github.com/Euro-Office/eurooffice-nextcloud.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Euro-Office is a microservice-style platform. The diagram below shows the high-level component layout for a typical single-node deployment.

flowchart LR
    Browser[Web browser]
    Desktop[Desktop editor]
    Browser <--> DocService
    Desktop <--> DocService
    DocService <--> Redis[(Redis)]
    DocService <--> RabbitMQ[(RabbitMQ)]
    DocService <--> DB[(SQL database)]
    DocService --> Converter[FileConverter]
    Converter --> Core[Core C++ engine]
    DocService --> Storage[(Object storage)]
    Converter --> Storage
    AdminPanel <--> DB
    DocService --> Metrics[(StatsD)]

docs.title: Backend services
docs.text:
Service Role Default port DocService Real-time collaboration, Socket.io transport, document session orchestration. 8080 FileConverter Wraps native C++ converters for format translation. — AdminPanel License management and system monitoring UI. 9000 Metrics StatsD-based metrics emission. —

All four are Node.js processes living in the server repository.

docs.title: External dependencies
docs.text:
• Redis — Session state and real-time coordination.
  • RabbitMQ — Inter-service messaging.
  • SQL database — One of MySQL, PostgreSQL, MSSQL, or Oracle.
  • Object storage (optional) — S3-compatible or Azure Blob; falls back to local filesystem.

A configuration section covering the setup of each will follow.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text: Editors are vanilla JavaScript with RequireJS modules — no React/Vue/Angular — built per document type and served as static assets. See components → web-apps.

docs.title: Native core
docs.text: A C++ rendering and conversion engine. Output artifacts are consumed by FileConverter at runtime. See components → core.

docs.title: Scaling out
docs.text: For multi-node deployments DocService, FileConverter, and storage scale horizontally behind a load balancer; Redis and RabbitMQ are clustered, and the SQL database is replicated. An upcoming high-availability configuration page will cover the topology in depth.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
Euro-Office is composed of several independent repositories. Each is built and versioned separately; the top-level DocumentServer repository ties them together via git submodules.

Repository Purpose server Node.js backend: DocService, FileConverter, AdminPanel, Metrics. web-apps Browser-based editors for documents, spreadsheets, presentations, PDFs, Visio. sdkjs JavaScript SDK implementing the Office Open XML document models. core C++ rendering and conversion engine. core-fonts Font assets bundled with the platform. DesktopEditors Native multi-format office suite for Windows, macOS, Linux. docker-base Base Docker image with build dependencies. docker-ci CI/build automation layers on top of docker-base. document-server-package M4-template-based DEB/RPM packaging. DocumentServer Top-level repository; aggregates the above as submodules. qa-acceptance Acceptance test suite for the platform.

docs.title: How to navigate the source
docs.text:
If you are looking for…

  • Backend code (Node.js) → server/
  • Editor UI → web-apps/
  • Document model logic → sdkjs/
  • Format converters / renderers (C++) → core/
  • Build & Docker image definitions → DocumentServer/build/
  • Local development environment → DocumentServer/develop/
  • Packaging (DEB/RPM) → document-server-package/

docs.title: Suggested reading order
docs.text:
If you are new to Euro-Office, read in order:

  1. Architecture — how the components fit together at runtime.
  2. Installation — production deployment paths in depth.

docs.title: Licensing
docs.text:
Euro-Office is licensed under the AGPL-3.0, inherited from its upstream ONLYOFFICE Document Server lineage.

For the full source license, see the LICENSE file in the DocumentServer repository.

Coming soon

This page will cover:

  • Commercial licensing options for Euro-Office.
  • The contributor license agreement (CLA) and signed-commit requirements.
  • Third-party component attributions and their licenses (FreeType, AGG, libxml2, Closure Compiler, etc.).
  • The binary-files audit that confirms source access for every shipped artifact.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Euro-Office is a self-hostable office suite combining a real-time collaborative document server, web-based editors for documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and PDFs, and desktop editors for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

It is a fork of ONLYOFFICE Document Server, maintained independently to ship on a release schedule and packaging story that fits sovereign-cloud deployments.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• Document server — real-time collaboration, format conversion, REST API.
  • Web editors — Word/Excel/PowerPoint-compatible editors that run in any browser.
  • Desktop editors — native applications for offline editing.
  • DMS-friendly — Nextcloud integration is first-class; the REST API also supports custom DMS integrations.

docs.title: What this documentation covers
docs.text:
• Installation across Docker, package managers, and native binaries.
  • Configuration of databases, message queues, storage backends, and TLS.
  • Integration with Nextcloud and other document-management systems.
  • Operating a Euro-Office deployment: monitoring, backups, upgrades.
  • Building Euro-Office from source and contributing changes.

docs.title: What this documentation does not cover
docs.text:
• Code-level API reference
  • End user documentation

docs.title: Where to go next
docs.text:
• New to Euro-Office? Start with the architecture overview.
  • Ready to run something? Jump to the Docker installation.
  • Building from source? See the developer setup.
